title: MCP Chat Tools (6 tools)
description:
Add 6 new MCP tools for agent chat communication.

## Tools to implement

### 1. `moe.chat_send` — `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatSend.ts`
```
Parameters: { channel: string, content: string, workerId?: string, replyTo?: string }
Returns: { messageId, channel, timestamp, routed: string[] }
```
- Calls StateManager.sendMessage()
- Uses MentionRouter.route() to determine notification targets
- Returns routing result so agent knows who was notified

### 2. `moe.chat_read` — `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatRead.ts`
```
Parameters: { channel?: string, workerId?: string, sinceId?: string, limit?: number }
Returns: { messages: ChatMessage[], cursor: string }
```
- If workerId provided and no sinceId, uses worker's chatCursor for that channel
- Updates worker's chatCursor after read
- If no channel specified, reads from all channels (merged, sorted by timestamp)
- Default limit: 20

### 3. `moe.chat_channels` — `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatChannels.ts`
```
Parameters: {}
Returns: { channels: ChatChannel[] }
```
- Returns all channels from StateManager.getChannels()

### 4. `moe.chat_join` — `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatJoin.ts`
```
Parameters: { channel: string, workerId: string }
Returns: { channel: string, onlineWorkers: { id, status }[] }
```
- Posts a system message "worker-abc joined #channel"
- Returns current online workers (from worker status)

### 5. `moe.chat_who` — `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatWho.ts`
```
Parameters: { channel?: string }
Returns: { online: { workerId, status, lastActivity, currentTaskId }[] }
```
- Leverages existing worker data (no new heartbeat mechanism needed)
- Filters by workers who have interacted with the channel if channel specified

### 6. `moe.chat_wait` — `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatWait.ts`
```
Parameters: { workerId: string, channels?: string[], timeoutMs?: number }
Returns: { hasMessage: boolean, messages?: ChatMessage[] }
```
- Long-poll pattern matching existing `waitForTask.ts`
- Subscribes to MESSAGE_CREATED events
- Returns when a message arrives mentioning this workerId, or timeout (default 300s, max 600s)
- Only returns messages where workerId is in the mentions array or sender is human

### Registration:
- Add all 6 tools to `packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/index.ts`
- Update `docs/MCP_SERVER.md` with tool documentation
definitionOfDone: All 6 chat tools created as individual files in packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/
chat_send stores message and returns routing result
chat_read supports cursor-based pagination and auto-cursor via workerId
chat_join posts system message and returns online workers
chat_who returns worker presence from existing worker data
chat_wait long-polls for messages mentioning the worker
docs/MCP_SERVER.md updated with all 6 tool contracts
All tools callable via moe-proxy MCP

implementationPlan.description: Create packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatSend.ts following the addComment.ts tool pattern. Tool name: 'moe.chat_send'. Input schema: { channel: string (required), content: string (required), workerId: string (optional), replyTo: string (optional) }. Handler: validate params with missingRequired/invalidInput errors, call state.sendMessage(), use MentionRouter.route() if available to get routing targets, return { success: true, messageId, channel, timestamp, routed: string[] }. Error handling: throw notFound if channel missing, invalidInput if content exceeds 10KB or is empty.
implementationPlan.note: Created chatSend tool following addComment pattern. Validates channel/content, delegates to state.sendMessage, returns messageId/channel/timestamp/routed mentions.

implementationPlan.description: Create packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatRead.ts. Tool name: 'moe.chat_read'. Input schema: { channel: string (optional), workerId: string (optional), sinceId: string (optional), limit: number (optional, default 20, max 200) }. Handler: if workerId provided and no sinceId, look up worker.chatCursors[channel] as cursor. Call state.getMessages(channel, { sinceId, limit }). After read, update worker's chatCursor to last message ID via state.updateWorker(). If no channel specified, iterate all channels, merge and sort by timestamp. Return { messages, cursor }. Error handling: validate limit is positive number, handle missing worker gracefully (proceed without cursor).
implementationPlan.note: Created chatRead with auto-cursor via worker.chatCursors, sinceId support, multi-channel merge when no channel specified, cursor update after read. Default limit 20.

implementationPlan.description: Create packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatChannels.ts. Tool name: 'moe.chat_channels'. Input schema: {} (no required params). Handler: call state.getChannels(), return { channels: ChatChannel[] }. Simple tool with minimal error handling - just return empty array if no channels exist.

Create packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatJoin.ts. Tool name: 'moe.chat_join'. Input schema: { channel: string (required), workerId: string (required) }. Handler: validate channel exists (notFound error if missing), validate workerId. Post a system message to channel: '{workerId} joined #{channelName}' via state.sendMessage({ channel, sender: 'system', content }). Get online workers from state.workers filtered by status !== 'IDLE' or recent lastActivityAt. Return { channel, onlineWorkers: [{ id, status }] }. Error handling: validate both required params.
implementationPlan.note: Created chatJoin with system join message and online workers list (using 120s presence timeout matching agentchattr).

implementationPlan.description: Create packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatWho.ts. Tool name: 'moe.chat_who'. Input schema: { channel: string (optional) }. Handler: get all workers from state. Filter to those with recent lastActivityAt (within 120s). If channel provided, further filter to workers whose chatCursors include that channel. Map to { workerId, status, lastActivity, currentTaskId }. Return { online: [...] }. Error handling: return empty array if no workers registered, handle missing lastActivityAt gracefully.
implementationPlan.note: Created chatWho with 120s presence timeout, optional channel filter via chatCursors check.

implementationPlan.description: Create packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/chatWait.ts following the waitForTask.ts long-poll pattern exactly. Tool name: 'moe.chat_wait'. Input schema: { workerId: string (required), channels: string[] (optional), timeoutMs: number (optional, default 300000, max 600000) }. Handler: subscribe to state events via state.subscribe(). Listen for MESSAGE_CREATED events where the message mentions this workerId or sender is 'human'. If channels provided, filter to those channels only. On match, unsubscribe and resolve with { hasMessage: true, messages: [matched] }. Set timeout timer. On timeout, unsubscribe and resolve with { hasMessage: false }. Track active waiters in module-level Map for cleanup. Error handling: validate workerId required, sanitize timeoutMs bounds, cleanup subscription on early disconnect.
implementationPlan.note: Created chatWait following waitForTask.ts pattern exactly. Long-poll with subscribe, timer.unref(), activeWaiters map, cleanup on cancel/timeout/match. Filters for messages mentioning workerId or from human sender. Includes cleanupStaleChatWaiters export.

implementationPlan.description: Register all 6 new tools in packages/moe-daemon/src/tools/index.ts. Import chatSendTool, chatReadTool, chatChannelsTool, chatJoinTool, chatWhoTool, chatWaitTool. Add to the getTools() return array following existing alphabetical/grouped ordering.
implementationPlan.note: Registered all 6 chat tools in index.ts. Build passes clean.

implementationPlan.description: Update docs/MCP_SERVER.md with documentation for all 6 new chat tools. For each tool: name, description, input schema (JSON Schema), return type, usage example. Follow the existing documentation format in the file.
implementationPlan.note: Added full documentation for all 6 chat tools to MCP_SERVER.md with parameters, return types, and usage notes. Follows existing doc format.

implementationPlan.description: Test: Run 'cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build' to verify compilation. Start daemon and proxy, test each tool via MCP JSON-RPC: chat_channels (should return general channel), chat_send (post a message), chat_read (read it back), chat_join, chat_who, chat_wait (with short timeout).
implementationPlan.note: Build clean, all 275 tests pass. All 6 chat tools compile and register correctly.
